name: Keep 3-CTA Residency And Amortize Barriers With Two-K Stages

summary.name: Keep 3-CTA Residency And Amortize Barriers With Two-K Stages
summary.hypothesis: Round 29 proved that the occupancy-first idea was directionally correct even though the end-to-end runtime regressed. The hot-band PTX branch dropped from 200 to 168 registers per thread, moved occupancy_limit_registers from 2 to 3, raised achieved warps from 16.59% to 24.77%, and collapsed long-scoreboard stall from 7.34% to 1.69%. The regression came from a new synchronization wall instead: barrier stall jumped from 5.19% to 10.97% and the hot-band kernel slowed to about 33.76 us. The cleanest next move is therefore not to undo the occupancy gain, but to keep the grouped_rows=4 and 3-CTA residency budget while routing the default hot-band launch onto the existing 128x128x32 two-K-stage kernel so each steady-state barrier protects two K tiles instead of one.
summary.expected_bottleneck: Synchronization and stage handoff overhead after the residency wall has been partially relaxed.
summary.risk: Moderate. This is still inside the same occupancy-first branch and should preserve correctness semantics more cleanly than reopening 256x128 ownership work, but it can easily give back the register win if the two-K-stage kernel compiles much fatter than expected.
metrics_to_recheck: end-to-end median runtime with a >0.15 ms improvement threshold over the current 25.911183 ms run
ranking_notes: This is the only branch in the repo that currently carries direct evidence of improved occupancy on the 128x128 hot band without correctness loss.
The latest measurement did not falsify the occupancy thesis; it isolated the next cost center as barrier/handoff.
The existing 128x128x32 kernel provides a bounded way to test barrier amortization without inventing a brand-new kernel family from scratch.
summary.stop_condition: Stop this follow-on family if it loses the occupancy gain from round 29 or if barrier stall stays near 10% with no runtime recovery; sub-0.10 ms movements should still be treated as noise.

notes: Node C may now implement this one candidate.
